passages.passage_text: Directions (Q. Nos. 45-50) Each question consists of a problem followed by two statements numbered I and II. Decide whether the data in the statements are sufficient to answer the question. Give answer (a) if Statement I alone is sufficient but Statement II alone is not sufficient to answer the question (b) if Statement I alone is sufficient but Statement I alone is not sufficient to answer the question (c) if both statements taken together are sufficient to answer the question but neither statement alone is sufficient (d) if statements I and II together are not sufficient and additional data is needed to answer the question

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Is D the mother of S? I. L is the husband of D. L has only three children. II. N is the brother of S and P, P is the daughter of L.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Among five people — A, B, C, D and E sitting around a circular table facing the centre, who is sitting second to the left of D? I. C is second to the left of A. B and D are immediate neighbours of each other. II. D is to the immediate left of B. E is not an immediate neighbour of D and B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How is A related to B? I. A is the sister-in-law of C, who is the daughter-in-law of B, who is the wife of D. II. B is the mother of A's son's only uncle's son.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Among five friends—J, K, L, M and N each of a different height, who is the second tallest? I. N is taller than M and K. K is shorter than M. II. L is taller than N. J is not the tallest.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Point X is in which direction with respect to Y? I. Point Z is at equal distance from both point X and point Y. II. Walking 5 km to the East of point X and taking two consecutive right turns after walking 5 km before each turn leads to point Y.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How many students are there in the class? I. There are more than 20 but less than 27 students in the class. II. There are more than 24 but less than 31 students in the class. The number of students in the class can be divided into groups such that each group contains 5 students.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: The sum of ages of M, N and O is 50 yr. What is N's age? I. N is 10 yr older than M. II. O is 30 yr old.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Satish, Anoop and Sandeep's salary is in the ratio 4 : 5 : 7, respectively. How much is Anoop's salary? I. The difference between Anoop and Sandeep's salary is double that of Satish and Anoop. II. Anoop gets ₹ 4000 less than Sandeep.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What is the difference in the ages of P and K? I. P is 20 yr older than M. II. M is 2 yr younger than Z.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How many ewes (female sheep) in a flock of 50 sheeps are black? I. There are 10 rams (male sheep) in the flock. II. 40 of the animals are black.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If a and b are both positive, what percent of b is a? I. a = 3 / 11 II. b/a = 20

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What is the ratio of male to female officers in the police force in town T? I. The number of female officers is 250 less than half the number of male officers. II. The number of female officers is 1/7 the number of male officers.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What is the value of n I. 3n + 2m = 18 II. n – m = 2n – (4 + m)

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If x and y are both positive integers, then how much greater is x than y I. x + y = 20 II. x = y²

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Is w an integer? I. 3w is an odd number. II. 2w is an even number.
